Patch seems generally good, but in case of serious corruption detected like this at least some console message should be printed to let the admin know of corruption, potential performance impact, and again when corruption is fixed and performance is back to normal.

If that is already printed somewhere that I didn't see, I will change the inspection result.

parents.subject: LU-957 scrub: osd-ldiskfs itable based iteration

commit.subject: LU-957 scrub: trigger OI scrub if found bad OI entry
commit.message:
LU-957 scrub: trigger OI scrub if found bad OI entry

If some RPC involves OI lookup and finds inconsistent OI mapping,
it should trigger OI scrub to check and repair the inconsistency.

Known issues:
When the fid is returned to client, the OI mapping corresponding
to such fid may be not updated, or not committed to disk yet. If
server crashed before OI scrub completed, then recovery with the
fid corresponding to inconsistent OI mapping may fail or blocked.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <yong.fan@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I9709386aa6d42954b619f6b1342adae59a2ec5a9
